content.text:
Here's a summary of the top 10 Hacker News stories:

## Top 10 Stories

| Rank | Story | Points | Takeaway |
|------|-------|--------|----------|
| **1** | **We do not think Anthropic should be designated as a supply chain risk** | 222 | Anthropic pushes back against potential government classification as a security risk. |
| **2** | **The Windows 95 user interface: A case study in usability engineering (1996)** | 131 | A classic look back at how one of computing's most iconic interfaces was designed. |
| **3** | **MinIO Is Dead, Long Live MinIO** | 200 | A bold industry prediction declaring MinIO's relevance in cloud storage infrastructure. |
| **4** | **Obsidian Sync now has a headless client** | 385 | Obsidian's note-taking app adds headless support for automated workflows. |
| **5** | **The happiest I've ever been** | 319 | Personal essay on finding contentment after a long period of struggle. |
| **6** | **Show HN: Xmloxide – an agent made rust replacement for libxml2** | 20 | A Rust-based XML parser aimed at replacing the aging libxml2 library. |
| **7** | **Block the "Upgrade to Tahoe" Alerts** | 128 | Tips for stopping annoying notifications from Tahoe encrypted storage service. |
| **8** | **Addressing Antigravity Bans and Reinstating Access** | 199 | A GitHub repository for helping users recover access to Gemini AI accounts. |
| **9** | **Building a Minimal Transformer for 10-digit Addition** | 33 | Deep dive into creating a tiny transformer model for simple arithmetic. |
| **10** | **Woxi: Wolfram Mathematica Reimplementation in Rust** | 244 | A Rust port of Wolfram Mathematica, promising more performance and flexibility. |

## 🔥 AI-Related Stories

- **#1** — Anthropic vs. supply chain risk designation (potential AI company regulatory concern)
- **#8** — Antigravity Bans and Reinstating Access (helping users access Gemini AI)
- **#9** — Building a Minimal Transformer for 10-digit Addition (research/exploration)

## 🛠️ Developer Tools & Open Source

- **#2** — Windows 95 UI case study (developer/design history)
- **#4** — Obsidian Sync headless client (note-taking automation)
- **#6** — Xmloxide: Rust replacement for libxml2 (library tooling)
- **#7** — Tahoe encrypted storage alerts (developer utility)
- **#10** — Woxi: Mathematica in Rust (development tooling)

Notable highlights: Obsidian's headless client and the Mathematica-to-Rust port are especially relevant for developers looking to automate workflows or adopt Rust-based tooling.
